items.headline: The Clarity Act isn't a ticket to sanctions evasion, actually
items.subheadline: The bill, as currently drafted, has the power to stop sanctions evasion at scale, argues Ari Redbord, global head of policy at TRM Labs.
items.author: Ari Redbord

items.headline: The privacy paradox of protecting kids online
items.subheadline: We don’t need to imagine the privacy pitfalls of age verification. They’ve been happening for years, argues Cardano Foundation CEO Frederik Gregaard.
items.author: Frederik Gregaard

items.headline: The Clarity Act is the most important consumer protection effort in years
items.subheadline: Consumers should not have to wait for another crisis to get the protections they deserve, argues Summer Mersinger, CEO of the Blockchain Association.
items.author: Summer Mersinger

items.headline: AI is destroying the internet. Math is our only hope.
items.subheadline: The rise of autonomous AI agents necessitates the utilization of zero-knowledge proofs, argues Brian Trunzo, chief growth officer at Succinct Labs.
items.author: Brian Trunzo

items.headline: Bitcoin hits one-month high above $66,000 as the chip trade turns back into a tailwind
items.subheadline: Asian semiconductor shares rebounded hard, a five-day ETF inflow streak passed $600 million, and oil pulled back on Middle East diplomacy.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: UK Parliament begins inquiry into banking chokepoint for crypto businesses
items.subheadline: The U.K.’s Crypto and Digital Assets All-Party Parliamentary Group (APPG) is focused on banks that refused crypto firms accounts or introduced restrictions on crypto transactions.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: Live markets: Bitcoin rises to five-week high above $66,400 on rising hope for Clarity Act
items.subheadline: Bitcoin ETFs have pulled in roughly $727 million over the past week, the most sustained stretch of buying since the record outflows of June.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: XRP price news: Ripple-linked token up 4% as traders watch breakout toward $1.35
items.subheadline: XRP pushed higher over 24 hours and tested short-term resistance, but the larger chart still needs a clean break above the $1.24-$1.28 supply zone to confirm a stronger reversal.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bitcoin, ether rally on Clarity progress report, Asian chip stock rebound: Crypto Markets Today
items.subheadline: Speculation that President Donald Trump agreed to a crucial ethics provision for the crypto market structure bill underpinned the gains.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: South Korea’s new economic roadmap is a massive bet on blockchain technology
items.subheadline: From tokenized public spending trials to government bond pilots, the state is aggressively pushing public finance onto distributed ledgers.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: The banking lobby is wrong about stablecoins and community banks
items.subheadline: Congress should not kneecap one of the clearest advances in payment infrastructure to protect community banks from a threat that has not been proven, argues Eco CEO Ryne Saxe.
items.author: Ryne Sax

items.headline: Ledger wants AI agents to manage crypto without holding your keys
items.subheadline: AI agents can read wallet balances and analyze portfolios but requires every sensitive action to be approved on a Ledger hardware device before it can be executed.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Bitcoin flat near $64,000 as oil hits a one-month high and Kimi AI selloff lingers
items.subheadline: Brent jumped almost 4% on escalating U.S.-Iran strikes, while Asian chip stocks stayed under pressure from Friday's Chinese AI shock.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Kraken launches crypto options, betting simpler products can expand the market
items.subheadline: The Wyoming-based firm launched USD-settled bitcoin and ether options, saying product design, not demand, has held back adoption.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Amazon Japan distributor AZ-Com Maruwa to adopt yen stablecoin JPYC for payments
items.subheadline: AZ-COM Maruwa will adopt yen stablecoin JPYC to pay 2,300 business partners and truck drivers. The move aims to speed up cash flow amid Japan’s logistics labor shortages.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Cross-chain protocol Allbridge halts after $1.65 million flash loan exploit
items.subheadline: The attacker used a $1.12 million flash loan from Kamino to manipulate pool ratios, enabling them to withdraw assets at favorable rates before bridging funds.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Hyperliquid plans to introduce decentralized prediction markets in HIP-4 upgrade
items.subheadline: Permissionless prediction markets will be available first on testnet and later on mainnet, Hyperliquid said.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Bank of Korea prepares for live CBDC transactions with 9 banks in September
items.subheadline: The Bank of Korea’s upcoming pilot will test live transactional rails for institutional deposit tokens to future-proof the nation's financial infrastructure.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Strategy (MSTR) raises cash reserves to $3.2 billion without bitcoin (BTC) sale
items.subheadline: For the second consecutive week, the firm kept bitcoin holdings steady, choosing to use the raised capital to boost cash for paying dividends on its preferred stock.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Ethereum news: Bitmine (BMNR) slows ETH purchase pace to shift cash to $86 million stock buyback
items.subheadline: The company added just 7,430 ETH, worth about $14 million, last week as it nears its goal to corner 5% of Ethereum's supply.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Hut 8, IREN land billions in new contracts, lifting AI compute stocks MARA, RIOT, WULF
items.subheadline: The deals sparked a rebound in AI compute stocks after investors questioned demand for new data center capacity.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Exodus (EXOD) to cut 25% of global workforce in payments shift
items.subheadline: This restructuring is part of a strategy to build a full-stack payments platform following its acquisitions of Monavate and Baanx.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: BTC ETFs attract $273 million in two weeks. That's peanuts compared to recent exodus
items.subheadline: Are Bitcoin ETFs back? New data shows $273 million in weekly inflows, but these are still "peanuts" compared to the massive outflows seen earlier this year. Explore why it's too early to call this a market reversal.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Crypto Week Ahead
items.subheadline: Your look at what's coming in the week starting July 13.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Crypto prices slip without a macro catalyst as PUMP steals the show: Crypto Markets Today
items.subheadline: Crypto drifted lower on Monday despite green equity futures and a flat dollar, with fear and greed at 34 and PUMP's 20% surge the only real headline in an otherwise quiet session.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: Inside Cardano's 'Van Rossum' hard fork, and how it matters for users
items.subheadline: The Van Rossem hard fork moved Cardano to version 11 on Saturday. And for the first time, Cardano's community voted in the upgrade, not the company that built it.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: DOG Mode vs. BIP-110: Inside the Bitcoin client built to bypass data restrictions
items.subheadline: While BIP-110 wants to restrict data through a consensus change and has almost no miner support, a new DOG Mode client wants the opposite and requires no vote at all.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Cardano hands core software over to outside developers to achieve full decentralization
items.subheadline: The move marks a major transition for the blockchain as independent engineering groups prepare to maintain the underlying software code through 2027.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: DOG Mode explains Bitcoin's next governance fight
items.subheadline: Leonidas' DOG Mode client challenges Bitcoin's default relay policies, reopening a philosophical debate over censorship, free markets and who really governs the network.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Inside Zcash's new node that targets Visa-scale privacy at 50,000 transactions per second
items.subheadline: The new Zakura client is the first live piece of a plan to take Zcash from roughly one private transaction per second to payment-network scale.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bitcoin’s quantum problem gets a recovery tool, but not for Satoshi’s 1.1 million BTC
items.subheadline: Project Eleven says it has funded a proof that lets a wallet's own key-derivation path stand in as ownership after quantum computers can forge its signatures. It runs in 243 milliseconds on a laptop.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Michael Saylor calls Bitcoin's new BIP-110 proposal 'a bad idea'
items.subheadline: Bitcoin bull Michael Saylor is coming out against BIP 110, warning that the "Reduced Data" proposal is a risky attempt to regulate the blockchain. Discover why Saylor believe this "cure" for network spam is more dangerous than the problem itself.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Moonshot AI IPO push follows Kimi, Alibaba AI releases that shook bitcoin
items.subheadline: Days after Kimi K3 rattled chip stocks, Moonshot is moving toward a Hong Kong listing at a $30 billion-plus valuation while Alibaba's Qwen goes open-weight.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin rises to one-month high of $65,700 before pulling back with stocks
items.subheadline: Markets await second-quarter earnings reports from key tech firms, which begin this week.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: What Robinhood’s recent layoffs say about the current state of crypto investments
items.subheadline: Despite the trading platform’s restructuring and the wave of crypto companies making cuts, signaling a late bear market, Altcoin Pro’s Horst, Anderson and Zhuleku explain there’s no reason to panic.
items.author: Ryan Horst

items.headline: Tokenized securities need competition, not gatekeepers
items.subheadline: Innovation thrives when investors have choices. For tokenized securities, Washington shouldn't pick winners before the market has a chance to learn what works, argues Patrick McHenry, vice chairman at Ondo Finance and former Chairman of the House Financial Services Committee.
items.author: Patrick McHenry

items.headline: Europe is closing the door on offshore crypto, but it’s leaving the riskiest window open
items.subheadline: MiCA was never meant to address the giant crypto derivatives market. That could pose a serious problem, says Patrick Gruhn, founder and chief executive of Perpetuals.com.
items.author: Patrick Gruhn

items.headline: Europe led on crypto regulation. Now implementation must match ambition
items.author: Richard Teng

items.headline: Collateral, not yield, will decide which stablecoins win
items.subheadline: As yield-bearing stablecoins race toward a $50 billion market capitalization, the industry is optimizing for the wrong metric, argues Artem Tolkachev, chief RWA officer at Falcon Finance.
items.author: Artem Tolkachev

items.headline: Age verification is the surveillance nobody voted for
items.subheadline: KOSA and Chat Control both retreated on their most-criticized measures this week, but both kept mandatory age verification, the quiet mechanism that turns anonymous browsing into identified browsing for all of us, argues Billions CEO Evin McMullen.
items.author: Evin McMullen

items.headline: The UK has finally shown it’s serious about crypto
items.subheadline: Several recent regulatory steps indicate the UK might finally stop dragging its feet when it comes to crypto, argues Wirex CEO Chet Shah.
items.author: Chet Shah

items.headline: Clarity Act negotiations: High-level White House meeting planned, sources say
items.subheadline: The most contentious piece of the crypto market structure bill is unresolved in the final weeks of Senate runway, and the president is expected to discuss it.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: U.S. Senate unanimously opposes clemency for FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried
items.subheadline: The nonbinding resolution passed without objection after Bankman-Fried asked for clemency, months after Trump pardoned other major crypto figures, including Changpeng Zhao and Ross Ulbricht.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: France orders country's internet service providers to block Polymarket
items.subheadline: The regulator cited concerns over addictive mechanics, a lack of self-exclusion tools, and a high volume of French users bypassing previous financial restrictions.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: The GENIUS Act turns 1: State of Crypto
items.subheadline: The stablecoin-focused GENIUS Act became a law a year ago.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Brazil’s securities regulator sets up task force with 60-day deadline for tokenization proposal
items.subheadline: The framework will address critical issues like official ownership records, private key custody, transaction reversibility, and system liability.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Clarity odds jump to 43% on Polymarket after reports Trump agreed to ethics deal
items.subheadline: Bettors moved the crypto market structure bill to a 43% chance of passing this year, up from a record low last week, though no bill text has appeared.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Who is buying bitcoin (BTC) as price rallies above $66,000: Crypto Daily
items.subheadline: The day ahead in crypto: July 21, 2026
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Twenty One Capital CEO steps down as Tether's plans to merge three bitcoin firms falls
items.subheadline: The proposed three-way merger between Twenty One Capital, Strike, and Elektron Energy has been abandoned, with Strike no longer participating.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: MoneyGram's CEO says blockchain works best when customers don't know it's there
items.subheadline: In an interview with CoinDesk, MoneyGram CEO Anthony Soohoo said that their blockchain strategy has evolved from early experimentation into a broader effort to modernize the company's global payments infrastructure.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Stablecoin bank Augustus raises $180 million to build a clearing bank for the AI era
items.subheadline: The firm, freshly valued at $1 billion, aims to replace legacy correspondent banking with always-on infrastructure that connects traditional payment systems and stablecoins.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Russia passes historic crypto rules to regulate trading and target foreign trade
items.subheadline: The new framework sets strict limits for retail investors and forces exchanges to register, but keeps the door open for using tokens in international business.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Crypto giant Galaxy sets up $5 million fund to future-proof Bitcoin security
items.subheadline: The company is backing developers to fix security blind spots before next-generation supercomputers become advanced enough to bypass blockchain protection.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Claude's Fable 5 just solved an 87-year-old math problem, and it matters for bitcoin
items.subheadline: Claude Fabel 5 disproved the Jacobian conjecture over the weekend, landing days after the capabilities of China's Kimi AI became the biggest force moving bitcoin markets.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Movement Labs files for Chapter 11 months after token scandal
items.subheadline: The filing comes after months of upheaval that included a controversial market-making agreement, an internal investigation into its MOVE token launch and a Binance ban tied to its market maker.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: White House pushes Senate Democrats to take 'historic' crypto Clarity Act ethics deal
items.subheadline: Without revealing any details about the actual agreement from President Trump on potential presidential restrictions, his White House is urging its acceptance.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Bitcoin (BTC) price rally faces real test at $68,000 as 'summer slumber' grips crypto, analysts say
items.subheadline: BTC has rebounded 15% from the July lows, but some analysts cautioned that the next move hinges on clearing a level where many recent buyers may look to sell.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Crypto Clarity Act still at mercy of ethics section as Democrats balk at Trump deal
items.subheadline: The White House has circulated some details of the conflict-of-interest limits the president agreed to with Republican negotiators, but talks are still underway.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Crypto lobby group TDC sues Illinois to block digital asset tax
items.subheadline: Illinois enacted a 0.2% tax on all crypto transactions last month, with the tax taking effect next year.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: BTC price news: Bitcoin at $66,300 as yen hits a 40-year low against dollar
items.subheadline: Semiconductor stocks led a second day of gains on AI optimism, while the yen slid past 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: AI models escaped OpenAI’s sandbox and hit Hugging Face. Crypto is where that gets dangerous
items.subheadline: OpenAI said the systems had their cyber guardrails lowered for an internal benchmark, but the incident shows how autonomous exploit chains could pose a deeper threat to smart contracts.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa
